steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a proper noun
Added ال to make it specific (the night), and ِ at the end shows it's connected to what comes after it (إِذَا يَغْشَىٰ - when it covers)
Added و at the beginning to connect it with what came before. This word starts an oath in the Quran, connecting to what follows it (إِذَا يَغْشَىٰ - when it covers)
This is the basic form of the word that points to a specific time
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to something that could happen multiple times or in the future. Here it connects to يَغْشَىٰ (it covers) to show when the night's covering happens.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to make it present tense (happening now), and changed the ending to make it flow better when speaking
Added the alif khanjariyah (ٰ) to show the extended sound

steps.m: daylight (the time when light flows forth)
steps.n: Added vowels and alif to the root 'flow brightly' to make the noun meaning 'daylight' - when light flows forth
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ن because it comes after ال
Added و to connect this word with what came before it
Added kasra at the end because it's being sworn by (like saying 'I swear by the daylight')
This is a particle that introduces a condition or time. It's usually used for future or hypothetical situations, unlike 'إذ' which is for past events

Adding ا at the end changes this from just meaning 'at the time' to specifically meaning 'when' for something that will happen. In this ayah, it refers to when the day appears/becomes bright (تَجَلَّىٰ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added a shadda (double letter) to make the meaning more intense, and changed the ending to ى which is common for verbs like this
Adding تَ at the beginning shows the action happens by itself

steps.m: it made itself very clear (when the day)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show proper pronunciation. This word describes what happens when the day comes in this ayah.
This is a particle that can be used as a question word or to connect parts of a sentence

steps.m: and what (asking about something in خَلَقَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this question with what came before it. This word is asking about what Allah created in خَلَقَ (he created).
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this is past tense. This word comes after وَمَا (and what) in the ayah, showing what Allah did.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Added a shadda (double letter) to make the word stronger and more intense in meaning
Added ٱل to make it definite. The final fatḥa shows this is the object of the verb خَلَقَ (created) in this ayah.
Added basic vowel marks to connect the root letters
Added ى at the end to make it a proper feminine noun
Added ال to make it definite (specific)
Added و to connect this word with 'the male' (ٱلذَّكَرَ) that came before it
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what follows it. Here it emphasizes سَعْيَكُمْ (your pursuit/striving).

steps.m: to strive or work towards a goal

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form a noun meaning the act of striving. This word is affected by إِنَّ which comes before it.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this refers to the striving of multiple people being addressed
Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show strong separation
Adding ى at the end makes it describe multiple different things (in this ayah, it's describing سَعْيَكُمْ - your efforts)
Adding لَ at the beginning adds emphasis, like saying 'truly' or 'indeed'. Here it emphasizes that people's efforts are definitely different from each other.

steps.m: to lead to, to head towards
steps.n: This is the basic form of the word, which comes from the concept of leading or heading towards something
Adding ا at the end transforms this into a particle that introduces a new topic or condition

steps.m: And so, as for... (introduces the condition that follows in مَنْ أَعْطَىٰ)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning connects this conditional phrase to what came before it. This word introduces the condition that follows about 'whoever gives'

This is a special word that introduces a conditional statement, pointing to anyone who does the actions mentioned after it (gives and fears Allah). It's like saying 'As for the person who...' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added hamza at beginning to show active giving and changed end to ى because of weak letter و
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show proper pronunciation

steps.m: to protect oneself or be cautious

steps.n: Root letters got past tense vowels (fatha) and alif maksura ending to mean 'he protected'
Added reflexive pattern to show doing action for oneself. The ت combines with ق using shadda for easier pronunciation.
Added و with fatha to connect this word to the previous phrase. The همزة changes to ٱ after و.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled د (shadda) indicates intensification of the base meaning

steps.m: and he strongly confirmed/believed (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Adds conjunction meaning 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic adjective form
Changed the vowels and added ى at the end to make it mean 'the very best' for feminine things
Added 'the' (ال) at the start to make it specific, with proper spelling using ٱ, and added a small alif (ى) at the end

steps.m: with/by the most beautiful/best outcome (object of وَصَدَّقَ)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how the action was done, connecting it to the verb وَصَدَّقَ (and he believed/affirmed)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning stronger
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by replacing يُ with نُ
Added سَ to show this will happen in the future

steps.m: and so we will make very easy
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a result of something before it

steps.m: and so we will make it very easy (for him)
steps.n: Added هُ (hu) at the end to show WHO it is being made easy for - 'for him'
Root letters combined with vowels to make the simple noun meaning 'ease'
Added ى at the end to make it feminine and comparative
Added لِلْ (combination of لِ meaning 'for' and الْ meaning 'the') at the beginning
This is a compound particle made up of multiple parts that work together to introduce a new topic or contrast

steps.m: and as for (connects to previous statement while introducing what follows)
steps.n: The و (and) connects this phrase to what came before it, while أَمَّا introduces what comes next (مَنۢ بَخِلَ - whoever is stingy)
This is a word that refers to people in a general way. In this ayah, it refers to the person who does the action بَخِلَ (was stingy).
Added a small ن (called nun) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word بَخِلَ

steps.m: to withhold or hold back (especially money)
he became stingy (connected to وَٱسْتَغْنَىٰ - and considered himself self-sufficient)
steps.n: Added vowels to show this happened in the past and describes someone's character or state. This word is connected to the next word وَٱسْتَغْنَىٰ (and considered himself self-sufficient) to show how the person both became stingy and thought they didn't need others.
The root letters are put into Form X pattern which adds the meaning of 'seeking' or 'considering oneself' to have the quality of the root
Added fathas to connect the letters and make the past tense verb

steps.m: he considered himself free from need
steps.n: Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show he actively considered himself this way. The ى at the end with the small alif shows this is a past tense verb ending.

steps.m: and he considered himself free from need
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous phrase 'وَأَمَّا مَنۢ بَخِلَ' (and as for him who was stingy)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the denial stronger and more emphatic

steps.m: and he strongly denied (بِٱلْحُسْنَىٰ - the good [reward])
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this action with what came before. This word connects to بِٱلْحُسْنَىٰ which means he denied the good reward.
Connected root letters with vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changes from 'was good' verb to 'best/most beautiful' feminine form
Adding the definite article makes it 'the' best/most beautiful

steps.m: with/by the best outcome (object of كَذَّبَ)
steps.n: The word 'best outcome' gets the prefix 'bi' (meaning 'with/by'), showing it's connected to the verb 'he denied' (كَذَّبَ)

steps.m: to be easy, to make something smooth or simple

steps.n: Doubling the س changes the meaning from 'being easy' to 'making something easy'
Adding نُ changes it to 'we' form
Adding هُ shows what is being made easy

steps.m: and so we will make easy
steps.n: Adding فَ (fa) and سَ (sa) shows this connects to what came before and will happen in the future - like saying 'and so we will'

steps.m: and so we will make it easy (acted on)
steps.n: Adding هُ (hu) at the end shows what is being made easy - like saying 'make easy IT' instead of just 'make easy'
Changed the verb into a noun by adding damma and sukun
Added ى with alif (ىٰ) at the end to make it a feminine word meaning 'more difficult' or 'most difficult'
Added ال (the) to make it 'the specific difficulty' instead of just any difficulty
Added لِ to show this is what is being made easy in the phrase 'We will make easy for him towards the difficulty'
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used in many ways in Arabic.
Added و at the start to connect this word to what came before it. In this ayah, مَا is being used to negate the verb يُغْنِى (will not avail/benefit).
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning

steps.m: he makes (someone) rich/sufficient (subject: مَالُهُ - his wealth)
steps.n: Changed from past tense 'became rich' to present tense 'makes rich' by adding يُ at the start. The word now shows how something (his wealth) makes others rich instead of becoming rich.
Adding ى at the end shows this is a normal present tense verb without any special mood changes
This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. It's like saying 'away from' in English.
Added هُ (him) at the end to show who is being moved away from. In this ayah, it refers back to the person whose wealth (مَالُهُ) won't help him.
Root letters م-و-ل changed to مَال by putting the و as a long ā sound (alif) to make the noun meaning wealth
The word gets a 'u' sound at the end to show it's doing the action (subject)
Added هُ at the end to show possession (his)
Added pronunciation helpers - the small و (ۥ) helps pronounce هُ and the ٓ (alif khanjariya) extends the sound slightly
This is a special word that shows a specific point in time. It doesn't come from a root because it's a basic building block of Arabic.
Adding ا at the end makes it refer to something that will happen in the future or happens repeatedly. In this ayah, it's connected to تَرَدَّىٰٓ (falls) to show 'when he falls'.

steps.m: to fall downward or be destroyed

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled د to show he did this to himself intensely
Added the alif maqsurah (ىٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound at the end
Removing the pause mark doesn't change the core meaning

This is a special word that adds emphasis to the statement that follows it. In this ayah, it emphasizes 'upon us is the guidance' (عَلَيْنَا لَلْهُدَىٰ), making it mean 'indeed, upon us is the guidance.'
This is a preposition that doesn't come from a root
Added a sukun (ْ) before adding the pronoun. This is a spelling change to help connect the words.
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'us'. This word is connected to إِنَّ (indeed) at the start of the phrase.
The basic past tense form of the verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'guidance'. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's a special letter at the end of words.
Added ل (laam) at the start to show 'for the purpose of guidance'
Added another ل (laam) to emphasize 'surely/indeed for guidance'
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a special kind of ى
This is one of the special emphasis words that makes what comes after it more certain and important. The shadda (ّ) on ن shows extra strength in the meaning.
The و connects this statement to what came before it in the Quran, showing these ideas are linked together
This is a pronoun meaning 'we/us/our' used for first person plural. It's connected to وَإِنَّ (and indeed) to show who is being talked about.
The ل prefix adds emphasis here because it follows وَإِنَّ (and indeed). Together they strongly emphasize that something belongs to or is for 'us'.
Forms a feminine noun meaning 'the last/final/latter (life)'
Added alif after first letter and kasra on middle letter to make it into a word meaning 'last'
Added ة to make it refer specifically to the last life (the hereafter)

steps.m: surely the hereafter (connected to لَنَا - for us)
steps.n: Added double لَ (لَلْ) at start to add strong emphasis, showing this is definitely true. The word connects to لَنَا (for us) earlier in the verse.
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and alif at the end to make the feminine form meaning 'first'
Added ال at the beginning to make it definite (specific)

steps.m: and the first (feminine) (contrasted with لَلْءَاخِرَةَ - the last)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word. This word is contrasted with 'the last' (لَلْءَاخِرَةَ) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together

steps.m: he made (someone) warned (about نَارًا - the fire)
steps.n: Adding أ at the start makes it about causing someone else to be warned. The warning is about the fire (نَارًا) mentioned right after.

steps.m: I made (someone) warned (about نَارًا - the fire)
steps.n: Adding ت shows that 'I' did the warning

steps.m: I made you all warned (about نَارًا - the fire)
steps.n: كم means 'you all' (when speaking to a group)

steps.m: and so I made you all warned (about نَارًا - the fire)
steps.n: فَ shows this happened as a result of something before it
Root letters ن-و-ر were joined together and the middle letter و changed to ا to make the word 'fire'

steps.m: a fire (object of فَأَنذَرْتُكُمْ - I warned you of)
steps.n: Added tanween alif (ًا) to show this is the object being warned about in the phrase 'I warned you of a fire'
Root letters joined with fatha marks to make the word for 'hellfire'
Adding shadda makes the burning more intense

steps.m: it becomes an intensely blazing fire
steps.n: Adding تَ shows the fire gradually becoming more intense by itself

steps.m: it becomes an intensely blazing fire (referring to نَارًا/the Fire)
steps.n: The ى with alif above (ىٰ) is a spelling variation for words ending in ى
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَصْلَىٰهَآ (will burn in) negative, so the meaning becomes 'will not burn in'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to burn or be exposed to intense heat
steps.n: The root letters tell us this is about burning or intense heat
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يَ at the start to show it will happen in the future, like changing 'burned' to 'will burn'

steps.m: he will burn in it (referring to the Fire mentioned in context)
steps.n: Added هَآ at the end to show what is being burned in (the Fire). The آ makes the sound longer for emphasis.

This is the base form of the exception particle that limits what came before to what comes after. In this ayah, it shows that only الْأَشْقَى (the most wretched) will enter the fire mentioned in يَصْلَىٰهَآ (will burn in it).
Adding the shadda (doubled letter) and alif at the end makes the exclusion stronger and clearer
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أ at the beginning and changed the pattern to make it mean 'most' of the quality
Added ٱلْ to make it definite. This word is the subject of يَصْلَىٰهَآ (will burn in it) in this ayah.

steps.m: The one who (points to someone or something mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence by referring back to something mentioned before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb

steps.m: he strongly denied/rejected (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows that this wasn't just a simple denial - it was a strong or emphatic rejection

steps.m: to be close to or turn towards/away from something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled ل to show he did this action by choice. The ي changed to ى at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this action to what came before it. The small alif (ٰ) at the end helps with pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
The doubled letter makes the meaning stronger and more active

steps.m: he is made to stay away
steps.n: Changed from doing the action to receiving it - يُ at start shows someone else is keeping him away

steps.m: he will be made to stay away
steps.n: Adding سَ shows this will happen in the future

steps.m: he will be made to stay away from it/her (referring to the Fire mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adding هَا shows what is being stayed away from

steps.m: and he will be made to stay away from it/her (referring to the Fire)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement
The root letters were given vowels to make a past tense verb. The first letter و changed to ت because it's a weak letter at the beginning of the word - this is a common change in Arabic verbs.
Changed to superlative form meaning 'most'. This pattern with أ at start and ى at end is used to show something has the most of a quality.

steps.m: the most pious one (who will be kept away from it - وَسَيُجَنَّبُهَا)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific. This word is the object of وَسَيُجَنَّبُهَا (and he will be kept away from it).
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that points to someone or something

steps.m: the one who (refers to the generous person mentioned in context)
steps.n: Adding ٱلَّ makes it more specific and definite. In this ayah, it refers to the generous person who gives their wealth to purify themselves (يُؤْتِى مَالَهُۥ يَتَزَكَّىٰ)
Connected the root letters together

Added يُ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. The ؤ is used instead of أ because it comes after a damma. The ي at the end is the weak letter from the root. This verb is connected to مَالَهُۥ (his wealth) which shows what he gives.
The و changes to ا after fatha due to Arabic spelling rules
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object (thing being given) in relation to يُؤْتِى (he gives)

steps.m: his wealth (object being given by يُؤْتِى)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show this wealth belongs to him. This word is what's being given in the phrase 'who gives his wealth'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added ت at start and doubled the middle letter (ك) to show doing the action to oneself. The ا at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: he purifies himself (subject of يُؤْتِى - he gives)
steps.n: Added ي at start to show this is happening in the present/future. The ى gets an alif khanjariya (ىٰ) at the end. This word describes the person who gives his wealth in this verse.
This particle is being used here specifically as a negation, meaning 'not' or 'there is not', based on the context of the ayah
The و connects this negation to the previous statement, showing these ideas are linked together
The root conveys the concept of oneness or individuality
Added tanwin kasra to make the word indefinite, meaning 'anyone' rather than a specific person
Added لِ at the beginning to show this person is receiving something. In this ayah, they are receiving نِّعْمَةٍ (a favor/blessing)
This root relates to presence or proximity
Changes from root to preposition meaning 'with/at/near'
Added هُ with an elongation mark (ۥ) to show possession - 'him'. This word appears in Surah Al-Layl verse 19, referring to someone mentioned earlier in the passage.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects 'with him' (عِندَهُۥ) to 'favor' (نِّعْمَةٍ), showing that no one has any favor from/with Him that needs to be repaid.
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connected to what comes after it
The root relates to comfort, blessing, and well-being
Connected the root letters with basic vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changes from a verb (doing the blessing) to a noun (the actual blessing/favor received)
Adds emphasis to the word by doubling the first letter
Shows this blessing belongs to or is connected to something else mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: to give a reward or compensation
steps.n: The root relates to giving rewards or compensation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to تُ at start to show someone/something receiving the reward (passive), and added a sukun (no vowel) on ج to make it easier to say
Added ى at the end to show it's feminine (for 'she' or 'it')
This is the base form of the exception particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before it
Added shadda and alif to complete the particle. In this ayah, it's excluding what comes after it (seeking the face of his Lord) from what came before.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ا at start and ت after first letter to show doing action for oneself
Changed the verb into a noun showing the action itself

steps.m: seeking (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Changed to show it's the thing being sought after, with special Quran spelling

steps.m: to face or turn towards something

steps.n: Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun meaning the physical face
Added kasra at the end to show this face belongs to رَبِّهِ (his Lord) that comes after it

steps.m: to care for and help grow/develop

steps.n: The shadda (ّ) doubles the middle letter to show this is someone who cares for everything completely
Added هِ ('his') at the end and ِ under the ب to show this belongs to وَجْهِ (face) in the ayah
Added أ at beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'highest' or 'most elevated'

steps.m: The Most High (a name/attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Added 'ال' (the) to make it mean 'The Most High' specifically for Allah. The special ending shows it's one of Allah's divine names.
This is a particle that shows something will happen in the future, like saying 'will' in English
Adding لَ makes it stronger, like saying 'surely' or 'definitely'

steps.m: and surely will (in the future) (connects to يَرْضَىٰ - he will be pleased)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this with what came before it. This phrase is connected to يَرْضَىٰ (he will be pleased), showing that someone will definitely be pleased in the future
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at the start to change from past to present/future tense. The ending changes to ى because it's a special verb that ends in ى in the present tense.

steps.m: he is/will be pleased (in وَلَسَوْفَ يَرْضَىٰ - and soon he will be pleased)
steps.n: Added the small alif (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound at the end. This word comes after وَلَسَوْفَ (and soon) which shows this is specifically about the future.
